tools.name: provision_number
tools.description:
Lease a temporary programmable phone number for receiving SMS OTP codes.

Returns the phone number (e164 format) and a session_id needed for all
subsequent calls. The number is reserved for your session for ttl_seconds.

Next step: use the returned `number` on your target service to trigger an SMS,

tools.name: wait_for_otp
tools.description:
Block until an SMS OTP arrives for this session, then return the code.

Polls the AgentSIM API for up to `timeout_seconds`. Returns the OTP code
and the message it was extracted from.

If the OTP does not arrive in time, raises a ToolError with advice on retrying.
Always call `release_number` after

tools.name: get_messages
tools.description:
List all SMS messages received in this session without consuming the OTP.

Use this to inspect raw messages or check if an SMS arrived before calling
wait_for_otp. Does NOT mark the OTP as consumed.

tools.name: release_number
tools.description:
Release a provisioned number back to the pool.

Always call this when you are done with the session — even on error —
to avoid consuming pool capacity unnecessarily.

tools.name: list_numbers
tools.description:
List active sessions, optionally filtered by agent_id.

Use this to check for leaked sessions or inspect what numbers are
currently active in your account.

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "get_messages" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: List all SMS messages received in this session without consuming the OTP. Use this to inspect raw messages or check if an SMS arrived before calling wait_for_ot…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Toxic flow: untrusted input can reach an external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: get_messages · Public sink: provision_number, wait_for_otp, get_messages · Destructive: provision_number.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: get_messages · Public sink: provision_number, wait_for_otp, get_messages · Destructive: provision_number

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
